---
title: "Singular Value Decomposition"
subtitle: "Bildkompression mit einer Gummiente"
author: "Numerik"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    transition: none
    background-color: "#ffffff"
    center: false
    margin: 0.045
    html-math-method: katex
    controls: false
    progress: true
    menu: false
    css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Die Gummiente

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div>
    <div class="big">Unser Beispiel ist ein Bild.</div>
    <p class="muted">Genauer: ein kleines Graustufenbild mit 13 × 13 Pixeln.</p>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas duck-large" width="13" height="13" data-duck="original"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Weißer Hintergrund, grauer Körper, dunkler Schnabel, schwarzes Auge</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Was hat diese Ente mit SVD zu tun?

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas duck-large" width="13" height="13" data-duck="original"></canvas>
  </div>
  <div>
    <p>Ein Bild kann als Matrix verstanden werden.</p>
    <p>Bei einem Graustufenbild entspricht jeder Pixel einem Zahlenwert.</p>
    <p>SVD zerlegt diese Bildmatrix in einfachere Bausteine.</p>
    <p class="muted">Dadurch kann man das Bild schrittweise annähern und komprimieren.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Was bedeutet SVD?

::: {.formula}
$$
A = {\color{#007aff}U}\,{\color{#ffb000}\Sigma}\,{\color{#ff3b30}V^T}
$$
:::

```{=html}
<div class="grid3" style="margin-top: 52px;">
  <div class="card" style="border-color: var(--u);">
    <div class="label">U</div>
    <p>Zweite Drehung oder Spiegelung</p>
    <p class="muted">Ausrichtung im Zielraum</p>
  </div>
  <div class="card" style="border-color: var(--sigma);">
    <div class="label">Σ</div>
    <p>Streckung oder Stauchung</p>
    <p class="muted">Singularwerte auf der Diagonalen</p>
  </div>
  <div class="card" style="border-color: var(--v);">
    <div class="label">Vᵀ</div>
    <p>Erste Drehung oder Spiegelung</p>
    <p class="muted">Wahl der Eingangsrichtungen</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

SVD steht für **Singular Value Decomposition**, auf Deutsch **Singularwertzerlegung**.

## Geometrische Motivation

```{=html}
<div class="stage row">
  <svg width="430" height="430" viewBox="-120 -120 240 240">
    <path d="M -82 0 A 82 82 0 0 1 82 0 L 0 0 Z" fill="var(--u)" stroke="var(--ink)" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M -82 0 A 82 82 0 0 0 82 0 L 0 0 Z" fill="var(--v)" stroke="var(--ink)" stroke-width="2"/>
  </svg>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div class="big">?</div>
    <p class="muted">Welche Transformation bringt uns dorthin?</p>
  </div>
  <svg width="430" height="430" viewBox="-170 -130 340 260">
    <g transform="rotate(-33)">
      <path d="M -138 0 A 138 47 0 0 1 138 0" fill="none" stroke="var(--v)" stroke-width="22" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M -138 0 A 138 47 0 0 0 138 0" fill="none" stroke="var(--u)" stroke-width="22" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="138" ry="47" class="thin"/>
    </g>
  </svg>
</div>
```

Wie wird aus einem Kreis ein verdrehtes Oval?

## Lösung: Drehen - Strecken - Drehen

```{=html}
<div class="stage row" style="gap: 34px;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <svg width="190" height="190" viewBox="-100 -100 200 200">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="68" class="thin"/>
    </svg>
    <div class="caption">1. Ausgangskreis</div>
  </div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <svg width="190" height="190" viewBox="-100 -100 200 200">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="68" class="thin"/>
      <g transform="rotate(-38)">
        <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="72" y2="0" stroke="var(--v)" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round"/>
        <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="-72" stroke="var(--v)" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      </g>
    </svg>
    <div class="caption">2. Vᵀ richtet Eingangsrichtungen aus</div>
  </div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <svg width="230" height="190" viewBox="-120 -100 240 200">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="96" ry="34" fill="none" stroke="var(--sigma)" stroke-width="8"/>
      <line x1="-96" y1="0" x2="96" y2="0" stroke="#8e8e93" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="7 7"/>
    </svg>
    <div class="caption">3. Σ streckt mit Singularwerten</div>
  </div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <svg width="230" height="190" viewBox="-120 -100 240 200">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="96" ry="34" fill="none" stroke="var(--u)" stroke-width="8" transform="rotate(-33)"/>
    </svg>
    <div class="caption">4. U richtet den Zielraum aus</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.formula-small}
$$
A = U \cdot \Sigma \cdot V^T
$$
:::

## Zurück zur Ente: Bild als Matrix

```{=html}
<div class="stage" style="display:grid; grid-template-columns: 0.8fr 0.25fr 1fr 0.25fr 0.95fr; gap: 24px; align-items:center;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="original"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Bild</div>
  </div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div id="duck-matrix" class="matrix" aria-label="13x13 Matrix der Ente"></div>
    <div class="caption">Matrixwerte 0 bis 255</div>
  </div>
  <div class="arrow">→</div>
  <div>
    <table class="legend-table">
      <tr><td><span class="swatch" style="background:#fff;"></span>255</td><td>weiß / Hintergrund</td></tr>
      <tr><td><span class="swatch" style="background:#969696;"></span>150</td><td>grauer Körper</td></tr>
      <tr><td><span class="swatch" style="background:#505050;"></span>80</td><td>dunkler Schnabel</td></tr>
      <tr><td><span class="swatch" style="background:#000;"></span>0</td><td>schwarzes Auge</td></tr>
    </table>
    <p style="margin-top: 18px;">Jede Zelle entspricht einem Pixel.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

Ein Graustufenbild kann als Matrix dargestellt werden. Ein Farbbild besteht typischerweise aus mehreren Matrizen, zum Beispiel je einer Matrix für Rot, Grün und Blau.

## Auf Bilder wirken Matrixmethoden

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div>
    <div class="big">Wenn das Bild eine Matrix ist, kann SVD darauf angewendet werden.</div>
    <p class="muted">Die Ente wird nicht als Form gespeichert, sondern als Zahlenmuster.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="formula-small">
    X → U · Σ · Vᵀ
  </div>
</div>
```

## Ein Rang-1-Baustein

```{=html}
<div class="stage component" style="gap: 22px;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div class="card" style="border-color: var(--sigma); font-size:1.45em; padding: 18px 24px;">σ₁</div>
    <div class="caption">Gewicht</div>
  </div>
  <div>·</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div class="vector-column">
      <div class="vector-cell-u">u</div><div class="vector-cell-u">u</div><div class="vector-cell-u">u</div>
      <div class="vector-cell-u">u</div><div class="vector-cell-u">u</div><div class="vector-cell-u">u</div><div class="vector-cell-u">u</div>
    </div>
    <div class="caption">blauer Spaltenvektor u₁<br>vertikales Muster</div>
  </div>
  <div>·</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div class="vector-row">
      <div class="vector-cell-v">v</div><div class="vector-cell-v">v</div><div class="vector-cell-v">v</div>
      <div class="vector-cell-v">v</div><div class="vector-cell-v">v</div><div class="vector-cell-v">v</div><div class="vector-cell-v">v</div>
    </div>
    <div class="caption">roter Zeilenvektor v₁ᵀ<br>horizontales Muster</div>
  </div>
  <div>=</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div class="rank-surface">
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#eee"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ddd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ccc"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#c8c8c8"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ddd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#eee"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f8f8f8"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ddd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#c7c7c7"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#b8b8b8"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#b2b2b2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#c8c8c8"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ddd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#eee"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d0d0d0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#bcbcbc"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#a8a8a8"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#a2a2a2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#b8b8b8"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d0d0d0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e8e8e8"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d6d6d6"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#c0c0c0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#adadad"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#a7a7a7"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#bdbdbd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d4d4d4"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#ededed"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e3e3e3"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d0d0d0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#c2c2c2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#bdbdbd"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d0d0d0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e2e2e2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f3f3f3"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f0f0f0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e3e3e3"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d7d7d7"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#d3d3d3"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e3e3e3"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f0f0f0"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#fafafa"></div>
      <div class="surface-cell" style="background:#fafafa"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f2f2f2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e9e9e9"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#e5e5e5"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#f2f2f2"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#fafafa"></div><div class="surface-cell" style="background:#fff"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="caption">Matrixfläche<br>Rang 1</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

Der Spaltenvektor **u₁** beschreibt ein **vertikales Muster**, der Zeilenvektor **v₁ᵀ** ein **horizontales Muster**, und **σ₁** gewichtet, wie stark dieser Baustein ist.

## Rang-1 heißt: sehr einfache Matrix

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas duck-large" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="1"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Approximation mit Rang 1</div>
  </div>
  <div>
    <p>Ein Rang-1-Baustein kann bereits die grobe Richtung der Bildstruktur erfassen.</p>
    <p class="muted">Für Details reicht ein einzelner Baustein noch nicht.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Mehrere Ränge addieren

::: {.formula-small}
$$
A \approx \sigma_1u_1v_1^T + \sigma_2u_2v_2^T + \sigma_3u_3v_3^T + \dots
$$
:::

```{=html}
<p class="muted" style="text-align:center; margin-top: -8px;">Jeder zusätzliche Rang-1-Baustein ergänzt weitere Bilddetails.</p>
<div class="row" style="gap: 34px; margin-top: 36px;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas mini-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="1"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Approximation mit Rang 1</div>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas mini-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="2"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Approximation mit Rang 2</div>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas mini-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="5"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Approximation mit Rang 5</div>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas mini-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="13"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Approximation mit Rang 13</div>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas mini-canvas" width="13" height="13" data-duck="original"></canvas>
    <div class="caption">Originalbild</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Warum SVD komprimieren kann

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div>
    <p>Die Singularwerte sind nach Wichtigkeit sortiert.</p>
    <p>Große Singularwerte tragen meist die wichtigsten Bildinformationen.</p>
    <p>Kleine Singularwerte beschreiben feinere Details oder weniger wichtige Anteile.</p>
    <p class="muted">Für Kompression behält man nur die größten k Singularwerte und die zugehörigen Vektoren.</p>
  </div>
  <div>
    <div class="sv-bar">
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:330px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₁</div></div>
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:121px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₂</div></div>
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:71px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₃</div></div>
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:67px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₄</div></div>
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:42px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₅</div></div>
      <div><div class="bar" style="height:30px;"></div><div class="bar-label">σ₆</div></div>
    </div>
    <div class="caption">σ₁ > σ₂ > σ₃ > …</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Kompression als Entscheidung

```{=html}
<div class="stage">
  <table class="rank-table">
    <thead>
      <tr><th>Behalten</th><th>Gespeicherte Bausteine</th><th>Erklärte Energie</th><th>Bildwirkung</th></tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      <tr><td>k = 1</td><td>1 Rang-1-Baustein</td><td>80.1%</td><td>sehr grobe Ente</td></tr>
      <tr><td>k = 2</td><td>2 Rang-1-Bausteine</td><td>86.9%</td><td>Form wird deutlich</td></tr>
      <tr><td>k = 5</td><td>5 Rang-1-Bausteine</td><td>96.4%</td><td>nahe am Original</td></tr>
      <tr><td>k = 13</td><td>alle Bausteine</td><td>100%</td><td>Originalmatrix</td></tr>
    </tbody>
  </table>
</div>
```

Weniger Daten, aber eine brauchbare Annäherung: das ist die Kompressionsidee.

## Kernaussage

```{=html}
<div class="stage grid2">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <canvas class="duck-canvas duck-large" width="13" height="13" data-duck="rank" data-k="5"></canvas>
  </div>
  <div>
    <p>Ein Bild kann als Matrix dargestellt werden.</p>
    <p>SVD zerlegt diese Matrix in geordnete Bausteine.</p>
    <p>Jeder Baustein besteht aus einem vertikalen Muster, einem horizontalen Muster und einem Gewicht.</p>
    <p>Je mehr Bausteine man addiert, desto genauer wird das Bild.</p>
    <p class="muted">Für Kompression speichert man nur die wichtigsten Bausteine.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function () {
  const clamp = (x, lo, hi) => Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, x));
  let duckData = null;

  function reconstruct(data, k) {
    const {U, S, Vt, n, m} = data;
    const out = Array.from({ length: n }, () => Array(m).fill(0));
    for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) {
      for (let y = 0; y < n; y++) {
        for (let x = 0; x < m; x++) {
          out[y][x] += S[i] * U[y][i] * Vt[i][x];
        }
      }
    }
    return out;
  }

  function drawMatrix() {
    const target = document.getElementById("duck-matrix");
    if (!target || !duckData || target.childElementCount) return;
    duckData.brightness_original.flat().forEach((v) => {
      const cell = document.createElement("div");
      const gray = clamp(v, 0, 255);
      cell.className = "cell";
      cell.style.background = `rgb(${gray},${gray},${gray})`;
      cell.style.color = gray < 130 ? "#fff" : "#111";
      cell.textContent = Math.round(v);
      target.appendChild(cell);
    });
  }

  function drawCanvas(canvas) {
    if (!duckData) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
    const img = ctx.createImageData(duckData.m, duckData.n);
    const mode = canvas.dataset.duck;
    const k = Number(canvas.dataset.k || "0");
    const values = mode === "original" ? duckData.original : reconstruct(duckData, k);

    for (let y = 0; y < duckData.n; y++) {
      for (let x = 0; x < duckData.m; x++) {
        const darkness = clamp(values[y][x], 0, 255);
        const gray = 255 - darkness;
        const idx = (y * duckData.m + x) * 4;
        img.data[idx] = gray;
        img.data[idx + 1] = gray;
        img.data[idx + 2] = gray;
        img.data[idx + 3] = 255;
      }
    }
    ctx.putImageData(img, 0, 0);
  }

  function renderDuckSlides() {
    drawMatrix();
    document.querySelectorAll("canvas[data-duck]").forEach(drawCanvas);
  }

  async function initDuck() {
    try {
      const response = await fetch("assets/duck_gray_svd.json?v=11");
      duckData = await response.json();
      renderDuckSlides();
      if (window.Reveal) {
        Reveal.on("slidechanged", renderDuckSlides);
        Reveal.on("ready", renderDuckSlides);
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Could not load duck SVD data", error);
    }
  }

  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", initDuck);
  } else {
    initDuck();
  }
})();
</script>
```
